steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify the hypotenuse
steps.content: Since angle $B$ is $90^\circ$, $AC$ is the hypotenuse. The problem gives $AB=24$ and says $AC$ is $13$ greater than $AB$, so $AC=37$.

steps.title: Write the same area two ways
steps.content:
The altitude $BD$ goes from the right angle to the hypotenuse. The area of triangle $ABC$ can be written using either pair of base and height:
  • Using the legs: $\frac{1}{2}\cdot AB\cdot BC$
  • Using the hypotenuse and altitude: $\frac{1}{2}\cdot AC\cdot BD$
Set these equal and cancel $\frac{1}{2}$: $$AB\cdot BC=AC\cdot BD$$

steps.title: Solve for the requested ratio
steps.content: Divide both sides by $AB\cdot BD$: $$\frac{BC}{BD}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{37}{24}$$ This matches the correct answer.
